mission.title: Install and Configure Envoy on Kubernetes
mission.description: Envoy is a cloud-native high-performance edge/middle/service proxy that helps manage microservices traffic efficiently. Installing Envoy allows you to leverage its advanced features for load balancing, service discovery, and observability in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Helm repository for Envoy to your Helm client.
```bash
helm repo add envoy https://cloudnativeapp.github.io/charts/curated
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Envoy using Helm
steps.description:
Install Envoy in your Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and create a namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install envoy envoy/envoy --namespace envoy --create-namespace --version 1.5.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Envoy pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace envoy
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Set resource limits for the Envoy deployment to ensure it runs efficiently.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment envoy --namespace envoy -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"envoy","resources":{"limits":{"cpu":"500m","memory":"512Mi"}}}]}}}}' 
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the Envoy service to access it from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/envoy --namespace envoy 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Envoy release from your Kubernetes cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall envoy --namespace envoy
```

uninstall.title: Clean up namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the Envoy namespace and any remaining resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace envoy
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration and resources for backup purposes.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace envoy -o yaml > envoy-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date with the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Envoy
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Envoy installation to the latest version available in the repository.
```bash
helm upgrade envoy envoy/envoy --namespace envoy --version 1.5.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Envoy pods to confirm the upgrade was successful.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace envoy
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your Envoy pods are stuck in a Pending state, check for resource availability in your cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace envoy
```

troubleshooting.title: Envoy service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Envoy service, verify that the service is correctly configured and the port forwarding is set up.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace envoy
```

troubleshooting.title: High memory usage
troubleshooting.description:
If you notice high memory usage, consider adjusting the resource limits set for the Envoy deployment.
```bash
kubectl top pod --namespace envoy
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Envoy will result in running pods in the 'envoy' namespace, accessible via the specified port. You should also see the Envoy service responding to requests as expected.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add envoy https://cloudnativeapp.github.io/charts/curated
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
